response.justification: Bogdan is a capable polyglot engineer with a strong software craftsmanship culture and broad systems exposure, but his background has very limited alignment with the core requirements of this role. The position demands deep Java and Kotlin expertise, JVM internals knowledge, and hands-on experience with enterprise frameworks like Spring, Quarkus, and Hibernate — none of which are meaningfully represented in the resume. His primary stack across recent roles is TypeScript and Node.js. Java appears only tangentially (robotic arms at Altom, C components at National Instruments on a C IDE). His IDE-adjacent experience at NI LabWindows is in C, not JVM-based tooling, making the technical fit too weak to proceed.
strengths: Broad exposure to systems-level programming (C/C++, DLang) and some Java use demonstrates ability to work close to the metal
Genuine interest in developer tooling evidenced by NI LabWindows contribution and multiple open-source test framework projects (Fluent-Asserts, Trial)
Strong software craftsmanship practices — TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD — consistently applied across multiple roles
concerns: No demonstrated Java or Kotlin proficiency at the depth required; the resume's primary language stack is TypeScript and Node.js
Zero visible experience with Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework
No JVM-specific IDE internals or tooling experience; NI LabWindows work was C-based and does not transfer to JVM toolchain development
